topic.name: Military Tax Relief Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing tax relief and benefits to military personnel and their families.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income payments made to members of the Armed Forces as enlistment and reenlistment bonuses.
Requires the Secretary of Defense to continue the payment to members of the Armed Forces who are retired or separated for disability due to a combat-related injury.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent penalty-free distributions from individual retirement plans to individuals called or ordered to active military duty.
The taxpayer election to include combat zone compensation as earned income for purposes of computing the earned income tax credit.

topic.description: This topic encompasses initiatives aimed at enhancing the academic, social, and emotional well-being of disadvantaged youth through community partnerships and strategic state and local programs.

strengthen their families and communities

examines the natural, cultural, recreational, and scenic values and characters of the lands identified

topic.name: Military and Environmental Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to military operations, community redevelopment, and environmental management.

Document 1: Man in the Sea Memorial Act - Authorizes the Secretary of the Navy to permit a third party to establish and maintain a memorial to honor members of the Armed Forces who have served as divers.
Prohibits the use of federal funds for designing, procuring, preparing, installing, or maintaining the memorial.
Document 2: Defense Communities Redevelopment Act of 2009 - Reinstates provisions of the Defense Base Closure and Realignment Act of 1990 which allow military departments to transfer surplus real and personal property.
without the requirement to assess the property's value.
Document 3: Amends the Healthy Forests Restoration Act of 2003 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior to implement procedures to ensure that not less than 600,000 acres of federal land each fiscal year are treated with mechanical treatments.
Directs the Secretary to initiate salvage activities on federal land affected by a catastrophic event.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to health care policies and insurance coverage in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to require the Director of the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to ensure that no multi-state qualified health plan offers coverage of abortion.
Preempts any state law requiring coverage of or funding for abortions in multi-state qualified health plans.
Document 2: Extends COBRA coverage (health insurance continuation benefits) until the earlier of: the first date in which an individual is eligible for coverage under an employment-based health plan.
Directs the Secretary of Labor to provide rules setting forth the form and manner in which prompt notice to individuals of such continued coverage shall be made.

topic.name: Federal Agency Oversight and Land Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal legislation related to computer security, educational funding for military-impacted schools, and the management and conveyance of federal land.

Document 1: Directs the Inspector General of each executive agency that operates a federal computer system that provides access to classified information or personally identifiable information to submit to the Comptroller General...

Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to revise Impact Aid program requirements for distribution of school construction payments...

Makes LEAs which were eligible for Impact Aid for the preceding fiscal year, and experience an overall increase in student enrollment, eligible for school facility emergency and modernization grants...
Document 3: Requires conveyance of approximately 44 acres of federally owned land administered by the Agricultural Research Service...
Requires the city and the Secretary to bear their own attorneys fees.

Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue a final decision on whether or not to issue a permit under the Endangered Species Act of 1973
Deems the permit to have been issued if the Secretary does not make a final decision within 90 days of this Act's enactment

topic.name: Taxation and Financial Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to taxation policies and the regulation of financial institutions to ensure compliance and stability.

Document 1: Makes permanent the tax rate reductions for dividends and capital gains enacted by the Jobs and Growth Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2003.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to restore the unified credit against the estate and gift tax.
Document 2: Amends the Financial Stability Act of 2010 to subject the Financial Stability Oversight Council to the Administrative Procedure Act.
Directs the Council every five years to study the impact of its determinations to subject nonbank financial companies to Federal Reserve Board supervision.

topic.name: Water Quality and Environmental Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving water quality and protecting ecosystems in the United States, particularly regarding the Gulf of Mexico and drinking water safety.

topic.name: Education and Community Support Initiatives

Document 2: Directs the Secretary to award renewable, competitive, matching grants to institutions of higher education, Indian tribes or tribal organizations, or nonprofit organizations to implement a comprehensive continuum of coordinated services and supports.

topic.name: Veteran and Tribal Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing support and resources for veterans and tribal communities in the United States.

Document 1: Amends part C (Medicare Advantage) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make budget-neutral adjustments in payments to Medicare+Choice organizations
to reduce the variation in Medicare Advantage payment rates among counties in the same state within a very large metropolitan area.

Document 2: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Transportation for FY2010-FY2015 for construction of ferry vessels and ferry terminal facilities.

topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at addressing specific issues such as conservation, tax reforms, and educational support.

topic.name: Legislative Proposals and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative proposals aimed at addressing social, economic, and environmental issues in the United States.

Document 2: Repeals the termination date in the Jobs Growth Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2003 for provisions reducing individual tax rates on capital gains and dividend income.
Document 3: Designates specified lands in Utah as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.

topic.name: Government Regulations and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts related to government oversight, security measures, and regulatory frameworks in the United States.

Document 1: Detainee Security Act of 2011 - Requires any administrative board or panel conducting reviews to determine the eligibility for the continued detention of a non-U.S. citizen detained on or after March 7, 2011, at the U.S. Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
Prohibits any detainee from being transferred or released to or within the United States, its territories, or possessions.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to establish a maritime career training loan program.
Requires the Secretary to establish a revolving loan fund consisting of receipts from the repayment of loans to cover the administrative costs of the program.
Document 3: Makes the Roadless Area Conservation Rule (and successor regulations) inapplicable with respect to National Forest System land in the state of Alaska.
